proposal_text: This project aims to develop an NLP pipeline to assist researchers in conducting systematic literature reviews. The tool will automate the screening of academic paper abstracts against predefined inclusion and exclusion criteria, reducing the manual workload of the initial screening phase. The system will use pre-trained transformer models to classify abstracts as relevant or irrelevant based on researcher-defined criteria. The tool will be evaluated on a benchmark of three previously completed systematic reviews where human screening decisions are available as ground truth. All data consists of publicly available academic abstracts. The tool will provide a confidence score alongside each classification to guide manual verification. The researchers will open-source the tool for academic use. No personal data is involved at any stage.

assessment.risk_summary: The proposed NLP pipeline for systematic literature reviews involves processing publicly available academic abstracts, but it is essential to consider potential biases and fairness in the classification process. The tool's reliance on pre-trained transformer models also raises questions about data provenance and model explainability.

identified_risks.explanation: The use of pre-trained models may perpetuate existing biases in the training data, leading to unfair outcomes.
The reliance on pre-trained models may make it challenging to understand how the tool arrives at its classifications.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Research must contribute to society and human well-being — discuss how applications may harm individuals, groups or society and ways to mitigate such harms (Mandatory)
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Discuss potential biases and fairness in the classification process.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Be fair and take action not to discriminate — consider and ameliorate biases the approach may perpetuate (Mandatory)
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Consider and ameliorate potential biases in the classification process.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Consider and ameliorate biases in data and biased code/model outputs (Mandatory)
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Consider potential biases in the pre-trained models and data.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Give comprehensive evaluation of systems and their impacts including analysis of possible risks of applications (Mandatory)
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Evaluate the potential risks and impacts of the tool.

charter_rights_at_risk.relevance: The classification process may perpetuate existing biases, leading to unfair outcomes.
The tool's reliance on pre-trained models may make it challenging for users to understand how the tool arrives at its classifications.

historical_precedents.lesson: Bias in AI decision-making can have significant consequences, and it is essential to consider potential biases and fairness in the classification process.
AI systems can perpetuate existing biases if not designed with fairness and transparency in mind.

recommended_mitigations.mitigation: Use bias-aware pre-trained models or develop custom models to address potential biases.
Implement model explainability techniques to provide insights into the classification process.

tier1_mandatory_actions: Discuss potential biases and fairness in the classification process.
Consider and ameliorate potential biases in the pre-trained models and data.
tier2_reflective_prompts: How can you ensure that the tool is fair and unbiased?
What measures will you take to address any potential biases or fairness concerns?
